rules.constraint: Native lowering needs every parameter type resolved; an unannotated parameter cannot be typed in Rust.
rules.guidance: Annotate every parameter of a hot function end to end with supported types (int, float, bool, str, bytes, list[T], fixed tuple/dict, Optional).

scope.pattern: function parameter without a type annotation

rules.constraint: Auto-discovered candidates must not silently depend on the shim path; only explicit markers opt into shim semantics.
rules.guidance: Mark the function @rextio.native explicitly if the shim dependency is intended.

scope.pattern: auto-discovered candidate depends on a runtime-shim native

rules.constraint: A per-iteration interpreter round-trip would erase the native speedup, so the caller stays on the fallback.
rules.guidance: Hoist the boundary call out of the loop, or mark the callee @rextio.native when it fits the subset.

scope.pattern: scalar boundary call inside a native loop, comprehension body, or while-loop test

rules.constraint: The function stays native; the note marks a known, documented deviation from CPython behavior.
rules.guidance: Review the documented divergence (docs/unsupported-features.md) and confirm it is acceptable for this function.

scope.pattern: direct-native function relying on a documented, statically attributable divergence (e.g. bytes.decode error type)

rules.constraint: Dynamic features have no static Rust equivalent; explicitly marked functions may ride the runtime shim instead.
rules.guidance: Move the dynamic behavior out of the hot path, or keep the function on the fallback (@rextio.exempt documents the intent).

scope.pattern: dynamic Python feature (dynamic attributes, exec/eval, reflection)

rules.constraint: A recognized external-accelerator decorator is the user's explicit opt-in to that tool's semantics; the function is excluded from native discovery and stays on the fallback route without a diagnostic.
rules.guidance: Keep Numba for NumPy/array kernels and @rextio.native for typed scalar code; combining both decorators on one function is rejected.

rules.constraint: A plugin-typed function compiles as a PyO3 boundary entry point; a native-to-native call into it would emit Rust with the wrong arity and parameter types, so the caller is rejected in this release.
rules.guidance: Call plugin-typed functions from Python fallback code, or inline the covered operations into the caller. A REJECTED plugin-typed callee reports RXT072 (dependency rejected) instead; tooling keying on RXT092 should also handle RXT072.

scope.pattern: native candidate calls a plugin-typed function

rules.constraint: Native-to-fallback calls are rejected, except the explicit scalar boundary-call path (see core/scalar-boundary-call).
rules.guidance: Mark the callee @rextio.native if it fits the subset, split the caller so the native core calls only native helpers, or rely on a scalar boundary call for explicitly marked callers.

scope.pattern: native candidate calls a fallback-only project function

rules.constraint: If a callee is rejected, its native callers cannot stay native either.
rules.guidance: Fix the callee's rejection first; acceptance propagates back up the call graph.

scope.pattern: native candidate depends on a rejected candidate

rules.constraint: The explicit accelerator decorator wins (decorator > plugin > fallback); the informational note only signals that an active rule-providing plugin covers a package the module imports.
rules.guidance: Remove the accelerator decorator only if CPython-exact plugin lowering is preferred over the accelerator's semantics for this function. The hint is import-based (the function body is not analyzed), so removal may simply yield a core-native or fallback function instead.

scope.pattern: accelerator-decorated function in a module importing a plugin-covered package

rules.constraint: Each iteration crosses the Python/native boundary; crossings count toward the per-function demotion threshold.
rules.guidance: Move the loop into the native function so the boundary is crossed once per call instead of once per iteration.

scope.pattern: Python loop repeatedly calling a native function

rules.constraint: The return type must be annotated and inside the supported subset for native lowering.
rules.guidance: Annotate the return type with a supported type; -> None is supported for procedures.

scope.pattern: missing or unsupported return type annotation

rules.constraint: The function compiles to a PyO3 shim that calls the generated Python fallback: behavior-preserving, not a speedup.
rules.guidance: Treat the shim as a compatibility path; extract the typed hot core into its own function for a real native speedup.

scope.pattern: explicitly marked function using classes, exceptions, context managers, async, generators, or dynamic attributes

rules.constraint: Identifiers must be expressible in Rust (raw-identifier escaping covers most keywords, but not all names).
rules.guidance: Rename the identifier to an ASCII name that is not a non-raw-able Rust keyword.

scope.pattern: identifier not representable in generated Rust

rules.constraint: The call runs in-process on the interpreter: values and exceptions are CPython-exact, containers never cross, and each crossing counts toward the demotion threshold.
rules.guidance: Keep boundary-call signatures to immutable scalars (int/float/bool/str/None) and batch enough work per call to amortize the crossing.

scope.pattern: explicitly marked native calls a fallback-only function with an all-scalar signature

rules.constraint: set[float] has no faithful Rust lowering: CPython's NaN-identity deduplication cannot be reproduced.
rules.guidance: Use list[float] and deduplicate explicitly, or keep the function on the Python fallback.

rules.constraint: Only the supported type subset lowers to Rust; anything else keeps the function on the Python fallback.
rules.guidance: Use supported types on hot-path signatures, or split the function so the typed hot core stands alone.

scope.pattern: parameter annotated with a type outside the supported subset

rules.constraint: A direct-native function may call only accepted native functions, supported builtins, and supported stdlib functions.
rules.guidance: Replace the call with a supported equivalent, hoist it out of the native function, or mark the callee @rextio.native when it fits the subset.

scope.pattern: call to an unsupported builtin, stdlib, or external function

rules.constraint: Only the documented syntax subset lowers directly (assignment, arithmetic, if/while/for, comprehensions, restricted try/except).
rules.guidance: Rewrite the hot path inside the supported subset (see docs/unsupported-features.md), or accept fallback and mark with @rextio.exempt to silence candidacy.

scope.pattern: statement or expression outside the supported subset, or an invalid @rextio.native marker
